articles.title: AN OPINIONATED AND GROUNDED SNAPSHOT OF SOFTWARE TODAY
articles.summary: Making sense of the technology landscape has never been more challenging thanks to AI-accelerated change. But understanding what matters and what it means for your organization has never been more important.

articles.title: Read Technology Radar Vol.34
CLAUDE OPUS 4.7
articles.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4.7, offering improved performance on difficult engineering tasks, stronger vision capabilities, and more reliable long-running task execution compared to its predecessor.

articles.title: OPENAI'S GPT ROSALIND
articles.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT‑Rosalind, a specialized model designed to support drug discovery and biological research through improved reasoning, tool use, and integration with scientific data sources.

articles.title: CODEX EXPANDS INTO FULL COMPUTER AUTOMATION
articles.summary: OpenAI updated Codex with background computer control, multi-agent workflows, and deeper integration with developer tools, extending its role across the full software development lifecycle.

articles.title: THE COMPUTER IS PERSONAL
articles.summary: Perplexity launched "Personal Computer," an AI platform that shifts the operating system model from manual instruction execution to probabilistic goal completion. The agent utilizes deep web research as its core foundation to autonomously evaluate reasoning paths and drive multi-step workflows. This architecture transforms the computer into an active orchestrator, eliminating the administrative friction of managing fragmented software tools.

articles.title: JENSEN HUANG ON ANTHROPIC, OPENAI, CHINA, AND DEMAND FOR INFERENCE TOKENS
articles.summary: Jensen Huang recently appeared on an interview where he discussed mostly familiar topics. However, there were three exchanges where Jensen said more than he probably intended. Jensen even lost his composure when discussing China and whether sales of chips should be restricted in the region. This post details each of the exchanges and notes other observations about the interview.

articles.title: WHAT I LEARNED THIS WEEK
articles.summary: This post contains rough notes on pretraining parallelisms, whether distillation can be stopped, Mythos and the cybersecurity equilibrium, Pipeline RL, and why pretraining runs fail.

articles.title: THE PR YOU WOULD HAVE OPENED YOURSELF
articles.summary: A new Skill and Test Harness help port transformer models to mlx-lm, streamlining contributions and reviews. This tool assists contributors by managing model conversion tasks and provides reviewers with agent-assisted PRs complete with comprehensive reports. It aims to maintain code quality and improve efficiency in an open-source environment where manual review cannot scale.

articles.title: 11 DAYS TO DIGITALOCEAN DEPLOY: THE AI INFERENCE ERA IS HERE
articles.summary: DigitalOcean Deploy is April 28 in San Francisco. One day focused on production inference at scale. Real customer stories, a fireside with NVIDIA's Kari Briski on agentic AI, and a first look at DO's next-gen inference products. Speakers from Character, Workato, VAST Data, Arcee, and vLLM. In-person seats are limited, so claim yours! REGISTER

articles.title: NOW
QWEN 3.6 AND AGENTIC CODING
articles.summary: Qwen 3.6 introduces stronger repository-level reasoning and front-end workflow handling, along with a thinking preservation feature that maintains context across iterations.

articles.title: INTRODUCING TERNARY BONSAI: TOP INTELLIGENCE AT 1.58 BITS
articles.summary: Ternary Bonsai is a 1.58-bit language model family offering improved performance with minimal memory usage. It outperforms 1-bit counterparts, scoring 75.5 on average benchmarks and achieving 3-4x better energy efficiency. The models, available in 8B, 4B, and 1.7B versions, provide flexible deployment across devices like Macs and iPhones under the Apache 2.0 License.

articles.title: SANDBOXED AGENTS FOR CODEBASE MIGRATION
articles.summary: This guide outlines a structured approach to modernizing large codebases using sandboxed agents that operate on isolated tasks, validate changes, and return auditable patches while orchestration remains outside the execution environment.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC EXEC LEAVES FIGMA BOARD AMID PRODUCT TENSIONS
articles.summary: Anthropic's chief product officer stepped down from Figma's board following reports that upcoming models may include design tools competing with Figma's core product.

articles.title: OPENAI TO SPEND MORE THAN $20 BILLION ON CEREBRAS CHIPS, RECEIVE STAKE, THE INFORMATION REPORTS
articles.summary: OpenAI will pay Cerebras more than $20 billion over the next three years to use servers powered by Cerebras' chips. The deal may also give OpenAI an equity stake in Cerebras. OpenAI has also agreed to provide Cerebras with about $1 billion to help fund the development of data centers. Cerebras is targeting an IPO in the second quarter of this year.

articles.title: FULLY REMOTE)
articles.summary: TLDR's Applied AI team is tasked with making every process at TLDR legible to code, runnable by anyone, and composable into larger workflows. Join a small, fast moving team using the latest AI tools with an unlimited token budget. Learn more [19].

articles.title: CHROME AI MODE ADDS SIDE-BY-SIDE BROWSING
articles.summary: Google updated AI Mode in Chrome to open webpages alongside AI responses, enabling continuous context while exploring, comparing, and querying content.

articles.title: VERCEL WORKFLOWS
articles.summary: Vercel announced general availability of Workflows, extending its framework-defined infrastructure model to long-running, durable systems with built-in reliability and observability.

articles.title: WINDSURF 2.0 ADDS DEVIN AND AGENT COMMAND CENTER
XAI PLANS TO SUPPLY TENS OF THOUSANDS OF GPUS TO CODING STARTUP CURSOR
articles.summary: Cursor will leverage xAI's massive infrastructure to develop advanced coding capabilities while providing xAI with a new revenue stream.
